mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales (RSAFS) indicate robust consumer demand, which drives GDP growth expectations and raises nominal yields as investors price in stronger economic activity.
failure_condition: If RSAFS increases but 10-year yield does not increase in 60% of instances over rolling 2-year windows, this hypothesis is refuted.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects tightening labor market conditions, which increase wage inflation expectations and push bond yields higher over a short horizon.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth rate and 5-day yield change have a negative rank correlation over any 2-year period, this hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) declines below 7%, the labor market enters a phase of accelerating wage pressure, which boosts inflation expectations and causes yields to rise over the next month.
failure_condition: If after UNRATE crosses below 7%, yields decline in more than 40% of 20-day windows over 3 years, this hypothesis is invalid.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 650,000 units per month signal sustained residential investment, a leading indicator of economic expansion that drives yields higher over a quarter.
failure_condition: If HOUST above 650 is not followed by yield increase in at least 50% of 60-day windows over 8 years, reject.

mechanism_narrative: With the federal funds rate at the zero lower bound (≤0.25%), a sustained rise in core CPI signals that the Fed will eventually need to tighten, leading to a term premium increase and higher yields.
failure_condition: If in ZLB periods, rising CPI is followed by yield declines more than 30% of the time over 20-day windows, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous positive momentum in industrial production (INDPRO) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicates synchronized economic strength; this broad-based growth pushes yields higher as real rate expectations rise.
candidate_trigger: Both INDPRO and RSAFS have positive 3-month log change
failure_condition: If the joint positive signal does not yield positive yield move in over half of 60-day windows over 8 years, reject.

mechanism_narrative: During periods when the unemployment rate is above 6%, further declines in UNRATE signal recovery strength, which boosts yields as the economy closes the output gap.
failure_condition: If UNRATE declines while above 6% and yields fall more than 30% of the time over 20-day windows, reject.

mechanism_narrative: In an accommodative monetary environment (FEDFUNDS below 1%), increases in industrial production (INDPRO) are particularly bullish for yields because they signal that low rates are successfully stimulating supply capacity.
failure_condition: If when FEDFUNDS < 1%, increases in INDPRO are followed by yield declines in more than 30% of 5-day windows over 3 years, reject.
